title: Mentor Guide — Day 13 — The Business Engine
coachingObjective: Help participants connect morning role-play insights to FEC Boxes 4–5 and a realistic weekly Business Engine.

timing.preSession: Review Deck 01 flow and blank Business Engine Canvas preview
timing.duringSession: Probe role-play reflections, FEC edits, and engine numbers for realism

discussionQuestions: What surprised you in the morning role play?
Why will clients choose this practice over another?
Can this weekly engine produce the business they want?
Do they have an idea — or a venture?
observationAreas: Single business lever selection on Canvas Page 2
coachingTips: Push for concrete moments from role play in Box 4
Ask: what happens if you run this engine for 4 weeks straight?
Encourage ONE lever — not three priorities
warningSigns: Generic client experience language with no role-play evidence
Weekly engine left at default 10–5–3–1 without personal targets
Year 1 revenue commitment with no connection to weekly engine
reflectionPrompts: What builds trust in discovery conversations?
Which business lever creates the biggest improvement?
expectedOutcomes: SPIKE Business Engine Canvas™ with weekly/monthly targets

activityCoachingNotes.note: Listen for empathy and question quality — not product knowledge.
Challenge squads to justify their weekly numbers; run growth simulation live.

debriefWithFaculty: Which squads connected role play to FEC most effectively?
Are engine numbers realistic for Week 3 interns?
